title: Facilitator/workshop guide for advocacy orgs
context: Advocacy organisations that want to run group training workshops using the primer need a facilitator guide — session plans, discussion prompts, timing guidance, and instructions for administering the pre/post evaluation instrument. This guide supports reuse and adoption (outcome metric 7: >= 3 orgs adopt the material within 12 months of v1.0) and lowers the barrier for an org to run the curriculum without the original maintainer's involvement.
objective: Author a facilitator and workshop guide for advocacy organisations wishing to run group training sessions using the Patient-Advocate-Research-Primer curriculum, covering session plans, facilitation tips, and evaluation administration.
acceptanceCriteria: Covers a recommended workshop structure for the full curriculum (e.g. a multi-session format) and a condensed single-session format, with timing guidance for each.
Includes discussion prompts and facilitation tips for each module, designed to surface advocates' existing assumptions and common misconceptions about research evidence.
Includes instructions for administering the pre/post evaluation instrument (task 303): timing, instructions to participants, scoring guidance, and how to report results back to the project.
Written accessibly for an advocacy organisation staff member with no formal research training.
Carries a CC-BY-4.0 licence so organisations can adapt it for their own use.
Carries a persistent 'education only — not medical advice' notice.
Methodology reviewer sign-off recorded: facilitation approach and discussion prompts are pedagogically sound.
Experienced patient advocate sign-off recorded: guide is usable and appropriate for the intended facilitator audience.
resources: PLAN.md (§ Success metrics — Metric 7: >= 3 orgs adopt; § Sustainability — forkability)
output: A facilitator and workshop guide (content/facilitator-guide.md or equivalent) committed to the repo under CC-BY-4.0, covering workshop formats, facilitation tips, module discussion prompts, and evaluation administration instructions.
